{"step_by_step_thinking": "The investigator is studying the association between two categorical variables: maternal intake of iron supplements (Yes or No) and continuous variable birth weights (in grams). Since the dependent variable (birth weights) is continuous and the independent variable (iron supplement intake) is categorical, an appropriate statistical test would be a t-test. The t-test can be used to compare the means of a continuous variable between two groups. In this case, the investigator can use an unpaired or independent t-test to compare the mean birth weights between the two groups (those who took iron supplements and those who did not). Chi-square test is used for categorical data, analysis of variance (ANOVA) is used when there are more than two groups, and Student t-test is a specific type of t-test. Therefore, the most suitable test is the unpaired or independent t-test.", "answer_choice": "B"}
